summary: Completed testing of currency masks (R$ 1.500,00 format) across all financial forms. All masks are working correctly - formatting on input and converting to numeric values for backend.
passed_tests: Contas a Pagar - Valor mask: typing '150000' → 'R$ 1.500,00' ✓
Contas a Pagar - Desconto mask: typing '5000' → 'R$ 50,00' ✓
Contas a Pagar - Juros mask: typing '1000' → 'R$ 10,00' ✓
Contas a Pagar - Multa mask: typing '500' → 'R$ 5,00' ✓
Contas a Receber - Valor mask: typing '250000' → 'R$ 2.500,00' ✓
Contas a Receber - Desconto mask: typing '10000' → 'R$ 100,00' ✓
Contas a Receber - Juros mask: typing '2500' → 'R$ 25,00' ✓
Contas a Receber - Multa mask: typing '1500' → 'R$ 15,00' ✓
Aluguéis Máquinas - Valor Caução mask: typing '1000000' → 'R$ 10.000,00' ✓
Contas Bancárias - Saldo Inicial mask: typing '1000000' → 'R$ 10.000,00' ✓
Contas Bancárias - Saldo Atual mask: typing '1500000' → 'R$ 15.000,00' ✓
Cadastros - Limite de Crédito mask: typing '5000000' → 'R$ 50.000,00' ✓
Backend receives numeric values (not formatted strings) ✓
Complete flow: Create conta a pagar with masked values → saved correctly ✓
critical_code_review_comments: formatCurrency function in masks.js correctly formats input as Brazilian currency (R$ 1.500,00)
parseCurrency function correctly converts formatted string back to numeric value
All financial forms use formatCurrencyInput/formatCurrency for input formatting
handleSubmit functions use parseCurrency to convert values before sending to backend
Backend receives and stores numeric values (e.g., 1500.00, not 'R$ 1.500,00')
seed_data_creation: Test data created and cleaned up during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Currency masks are fully implemented and working across all financial forms. The formatCurrency function in /app/frontend/src/utils/masks.js handles input formatting, and parseCurrency converts back to numeric values for backend storage.

backend_numeric_values.description: Backend receives numeric values, not formatted strings
